allow:
Bash(cat > /tmp/molt_socket_audit.txt << 'EOF'
================================================================================
COMPREHENSIVE SOCKET MODULE COVERAGE AUDIT - MOLT vs CPython 3.12+
================================================================================

Date: 2026-02-22
Status: INITIAL AUDIT
Scope: All CPython 3.12+ socket module public API

================================================================================
1. MODULE-LEVEL FUNCTIONS & CALLABLES
================================================================================

COMPLETE \(19 functions\):
  - socket\(\) - creates socket instances
  - socketpair\(\) - creates paired sockets \(Unix default, loopback TCP on Windows\)
  - fromfd\(\) - creates socket from file descriptor
  - getaddrinfo\(\) - DNS/service resolution
  - getnameinfo\(\) - reverse DNS lookup
  - getfqdn\(\) - fully qualified domain name resolution
  - gethostbyname\(\) - legacy DNS lookup
  - gethostbyaddr\(\) - reverse IP lookup
  - getfqdn\(\) - FQDN resolution
  - getservbyname\(\) - service port lookup
  - getservbyport\(\) - service name lookup
  - inet_aton\(\) - IPv4 string to binary conversion
  - inet_pton\(\) - IPv4/IPv6 string to binary conversion  
  - inet_ntoa\(\) - binary to IPv4 string conversion
  - inet_ntop\(\) - binary to IPv4/IPv6 string conversion
  - htons\(\) / ntohs\(\) / htonl\(\) / ntohl\(\) - byte order conversion
  - getdefaulttimeout\(\) / setdefaulttimeout\(\) - global timeout management
  - create_connection\(\) - TCP connection helper
  - create_server\(\) - TCP server socket helper
  - has_dualstack_ipv6\(\) - IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack support detection

MISSING - NOT EXPORTED \(6 functions, 4 platform-specific\):
  - close\(\) - module-level socket close \(redundant with sock.close\(\), but exported in CPython\)
  - gethostbyname_ex\(\) - extended gethostbyname returning aliases & addresses
  - getprotobyname\(\) - protocol name to number lookup
  - CMSG_LEN\(\) - auxiliary message space calculation \(Unix only\)
  - CMSG_SPACE\(\) - auxiliary message space with padding \(Unix only\)
  - if_nameindex\(\) - network interface enumeration \(Unix only\)
  - if_nametoindex\(\) - interface name to index \(Unix only\)
  - if_indextoname\(\) - interface index to name \(Unix only\)
  - sethostname\(\) - set system hostname \(Unix only, SECURITY: admin-only\)
  - send_fds\(\) / recv_fds\(\) - file descriptor passing over Unix sockets \(Unix only, but marked COMPLETE below\)
  
PARTIALLY COMPLETE \(2 functions - exported but stubbed\):
  - send_fds\(\) - exported in __all__, intrinsic implemented, but needs testing
  - recv_fds\(\) - exported in __all__, intrinsic implemented, but needs testing

STUB/NOT IMPLEMENTED \(1 function\):
  - fromshare\(\) - Windows-only, reconstructs socket from WSAPROTOCOL_INFO
    Status: Not applicable on Unix, Windows support TBD

EXCEPTION CLASSES \(3 - COMPLETE\):
  - error \(alias for OSError\)
  - gaierror \(address/name resolution errors\)
  - herror \(host/name resolution errors\)  
  - timeout \(alias for TimeoutError\)

================================================================================
2. SOCKET CLASS METHODS
================================================================================

COMPLETE \(27 methods\):
  - __init__\(\) - socket creation
  - __enter__\(\) / __exit__\(\) - context manager
  - accept\(\) - accept client connection
  - bind\(\) - bind to address
  - close\(\) - close socket
  - connect\(\) - initiate connection
  - connect_ex\(\) - connect with error code return instead of exception
  - detach\(\) - detach and get file descriptor
  - dup\(\) - duplicate socket
  - fileno\(\) - get file descriptor number
  - getblocking\(\) - check blocking mode
  - getpeername\(\) - get peer address
  - getsockname\(\) - get local address
  - getsockopt\(\) - get socket option value
  - listen\(\) - mark as listening socket
  - makefile\(\) - create file-like object for socket
  - recv\(\) - receive data
  - recv_into\(\) - receive into buffer
  - recvfrom\(\) - receive with source address
  - recvfrom_into\(\) - receive into buffer with source address
  - recvmsg\(\) - receive with ancillary data
  - recvmsg_into\(\) - receive with ancillary into buffer
  - send\(\) - send data
  - sendall\(\) - send all data
  - sendto\(\) - send to address
  - sendmsg\(\) - send with ancillary data
  - setsockopt\(\) - set socket option
  - setblocking\(\) - set blocking mode
  - settimeout\(\) - set timeout
  - gettimeout\(\) - get timeout value
  - shutdown\(\) - shut down connection direction

MISSING \(3 methods\):
  - share\(\) - Windows-only, returns WSAPROTOCOL_INFO bytes for process sharing
    \(Not applicable on Unix\)
  - sendfile\(\) - efficient file transmission \(platform-specific, optional\)
    Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED
  - sendmsg_afalg\(\) - cryptographic socket operations via AF_ALG
    Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED, very specialized, requires Linux ALG support

EXTRA/ENHANCED \(6 methods in Molt, not core socket API\):
  - get_inheritable\(\) / set_inheritable\(\) - file descriptor inheritance control
    Status: COMPLETE, tested via inheritable_dup_fromfd tests
  - read\(\) / readline\(\) / write\(\) - file-like interface via makefile\(\)
    Status: COMPLETE, part of SocketFile class in Molt

================================================================================
3. ENUM CLASSES
================================================================================

MISSING \(3 enum classes\):
  - AddressFamily - IntEnum for AF_* constants
    Status: NOT EXPORTED as distinct enum class, but AF_* constants available
  - SocketKind - IntEnum for SOCK_* constants
    Status: NOT EXPORTED as distinct enum class, but SOCK_* constants available
  - MsgFlag - IntFlag for MSG_* constants \(rare usage\)
    Status: NOT EXPORTED as distinct enum class, but MSG_* constants available
  - AddressInfo - NamedTuple for getaddrinfo results \(Python 3.8+\)
    Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED as structured type

IMPACT: Low - applications can use raw integer constants, but type hints suffer

================================================================================
4. SOCKET MODULE CONSTANTS
================================================================================

COMPLETE \(197+ integer constants\):
  Address Family \(AF_*\): AF_UNIX, AF_INET, AF_INET6, AF_UNSPEC, AF_APPLETALK,
    AF_DECnet, AF_IPX, AF_LINK, AF_ROUTE, AF_SNA, AF_SYSTEM
  
  Socket Type \(SOCK_*\): SOCK_STREAM, SOCK_DGRAM, SOCK_RAW, SOCK_RDM, SOCK_SEQPACKET
  
  Protocol \(IPPROTO_*\): IPPROTO_IP, IPPROTO_ICMP, IPPROTO_TCP, IPPROTO_UDP,
    IPPROTO_IPV6, IPPROTO_RAW, IPPROTO_AH, IPPROTO_ESP, etc. \(~25 constants\)
  
  Socket Options \(SOL_*\): SOL_SOCKET, SOL_IP, SOL_TCP, SOL_UDP
  
  Socket Option Names \(SO_*\): SO_DEBUG, SO_REUSEADDR, SO_TYPE, SO_ERROR,
    SO_DONTROUTE, SO_BROADCAST, SO_SNDBUF, SO_RCVBUF, SO_KEEPALIVE, SO_OOBINLINE,
    SO_LINGER, SO_RCVLOWAT, SO_SNDLOWAT, SO_RCVTIMEO, SO_SNDTIMEO, SO_REUSEPORT,
    SO_BINDTODEVICE, SO_ACCEPTCONN, SO_USELOOPBACK
  
  Message Flags \(MSG_*\): MSG_OOB, MSG_PEEK, MSG_DONTROUTE, MSG_DONTWAIT,
    MSG_EOR, MSG_TRUNC, MSG_CTRUNC, MSG_WAITALL, MSG_NOSIGNAL, MSG_EOF
  
  Address Info Flags \(AI_*\): AI_PASSIVE, AI_CANONNAME, AI_NUMERICHOST,
    AI_NUMERICSERV, AI_V4MAPPED, AI_ALL, AI_ADDRCONFIG, AI_V4MAPPED_CFG,
    AI_DEFAULT, AI_MASK
  
  Name Info Flags \(NI_*\): NI_NOFQDN, NI_NUMERICHOST, NI_NAMEREQD, NI_NUMERICSERV,
    NI_DGRAM, NI_MAXHOST, NI_MAXSERV
  
  EAI Error Codes \(EAI_*\): EAI_ADDRFAMILY, EAI_AGAIN, EAI_BADFLAGS, EAI_BADHINTS,
    EAI_FAIL, EAI_FAMILY, EAI_MEMORY, EAI_NODATA, EAI_NONAME, EAI_OVERFLOW,
    EAI_PROTOCOL, EAI_SERVICE, EAI_SOCKTYPE, EAI_SYSTEM, EAI_MAX
  
  Shutdown Constants \(SHUT_*\): SHUT_RD, SHUT_WR, SHUT_RDWR
  
  IPv4/IPv6 Options \(IP_*, IPV6_*\): IP_TOS, IP_TTL, IP_MULTICAST_TTL,
    IP_MULTICAST_LOOP, IP_MULTICAST_IF, IPV6_UNICAST_HOPS, IPV6_MULTICAST_HOPS,
    IPV6_MULTICAST_IF, IPV6_MULTICAST_LOOP, IPV6_V6ONLY, IPV6_CHECKSUM, etc.
  
  TCP Options \(TCP_*\): TCP_NODELAY, TCP_KEEPALIVE, TCP_KEEPCNT, TCP_KEEPINTVL,
    TCP_MAXSEG, TCP_FASTOPEN, TCP_NOTSENT_LOWAT, TCP_CONNECTION_INFO
  
  Miscellaneous: INADDR_ANY, INADDR_LOOPBACK, INADDR_BROADCAST, INADDR_NONE,
    INADDR_ALLHOSTS_GROUP, INADDR_UNSPEC_GROUP, INADDR_MAX_LOCAL_GROUP,
    SCM_RIGHTS, SCM_CREDS, SOMAXCONN, IPPORT_RESERVED, IPPORT_USERRESERVED,
    ETHERTYPE_ARP, ETHERTYPE_IP, ETHERTYPE_IPV6, ETHERTYPE_VLAN, etc.

All constants are dynamically injected from molt_socket_constants\(\) intrinsic.

================================================================================
5. INTERNAL _SOCKET MODULE
================================================================================

The _socket module \(CPython internals\) is implemented as a shim in Molt:
  src/molt/stdlib/_socket.py

EXPORTS \(matching CPython _socket internals\):
  - All integer constants from socket module \(via loop on molt_socket_constants\)
  - socket \(class, re-exported from main socket module\)
  - SocketType \(alias for socket class\)
  - error \(OSError\)
  - timeout \(TimeoutError\)
  - gaierror, herror
  - has_ipv6 \(boolean\)
  - CAPI \(stub, returns _PyCapsuleStub for compatibility\)
  
IMPLEMENTED CALLABLES:
  - CMSG_LEN\(\) - via molt_socket_cmsg_len intrinsic
  - CMSG_SPACE\(\) - via molt_socket_cmsg_space intrinsic
  - close\(\) - via molt_os_close intrinsic \(Unix fd close\)
  - dup\(\) - via molt_os_dup intrinsic \(Unix fd dup\)
  - getaddrinfo\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - getdefaulttimeout\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - gethostbyaddr\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - gethostbyname\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - gethostbyname_ex\(\) - via molt_socket_gethostbyname_ex intrinsic
  - gethostname\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - getnameinfo\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - getprotobyname\(\) - via molt_socket_getprotobyname intrinsic
  - getservbyname\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - getservbyport\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - htonl\(\) / htons\(\) / ntohl\(\) / ntohs\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - if_indextoname\(\) - via molt_socket_if_indextoname intrinsic
  - if_nameindex\(\) - via molt_socket_if_nameindex intrinsic
  - if_nametoindex\(\) - via molt_socket_if_nametoindex intrinsic
  - inet_aton\(\) / inet_ntoa\(\) / inet_pton\(\) / inet_ntop\(\) - delegated
  - setdefaulttimeout\(\) - delegated to socket module
  - sethostname\(\) - STUBBED, raises RuntimeError\("not implemented in Molt runtime yet"\)
  - socketpair\(\) - delegated to socket module

STATUS: _socket module provides necessary compatibility layer, well-structured

================================================================================
6. RUST INTRINSIC IMPLEMENTATIONS
================================================================================

SOCKET CREATION & LIFECYCLE:
  ✓ molt_socket_new\(\) - create socket with family/type/proto
  ✓ molt_socket_clone\(\) - duplicate socket \(for dup\(\)\)
  ✓ molt_socket_close\(\) - close socket handle
  ✓ molt_socket_drop\(\) - deallocate socket resource
  ✓ molt_socket_fileno\(\) - get file descriptor

TIMEOUT & BLOCKING:
  ✓ molt_socket_gettimeout\(\) - get socket timeout
  ✓ molt_socket_settimeout\(\) - set socket timeout
  ✓ molt_socket_getblocking\(\) - check blocking mode
  ✓ molt_socket_setblocking\(\) - set blocking mode

BINDING & LISTENING:
  ✓ molt_socket_bind\(\) - bind to address
  ✓ molt_socket_listen\(\) - mark as listener
  ✓ molt_socket_accept\(\) - accept connection

CONNECTION:
  ✓ molt_socket_connect\(\) - initiate connection \(blocking\)
  ✓ molt_socket_connect_ex\(\) - connect returning error code

DATA TRANSFER - BASIC:
  ✓ molt_socket_recv\(\) - receive data with flags
  ✓ molt_socket_recv_into\(\) - receive into buffer
  ✓ molt_socket_send\(\) - send data with flags
  ✓ molt_socket_sendall\(\) - send all data
  ✓ molt_socket_recvfrom\(\) - receive with source address
  ✓ molt_socket_recvfrom_into\(\) - receive into buffer with address
  ✓ molt_socket_sendto\(\) - send to address

DATA TRANSFER - ADVANCED:
  ✓ molt_socket_recvmsg\(\) - receive with ancillary data
  ✓ molt_socket_recvmsg_into\(\) - receive ancillary into buffer
  ✓ molt_socket_sendmsg\(\) - send with ancillary data
  ✓ molt_socket_send_fds\(\) - send file descriptors \(Unix\)
  ✓ molt_socket_recv_fds\(\) - receive file descriptors \(Unix\)

SOCKET OPTIONS:
  ✓ molt_socket_setsockopt\(\) - set socket option
  ✓ molt_socket_getsockopt\(\) - get socket option
  ✓ molt_socket_shutdown\(\) - shutdown connection

ADDRESS INFORMATION:
  ✓ molt_socket_getsockname\(\) - get local address
  ✓ molt_socket_getpeername\(\) - get peer address
  ✓ molt_socket_detach\(\) - detach and return fd

NAME RESOLUTION:
  ✓ molt_socket_getaddrinfo\(\) - DNS/service resolution
  ✓ molt_socket_getnameinfo\(\) - reverse DNS
  ✓ molt_socket_gethostname\(\) - get system hostname
  ✓ molt_socket_gethostbyname\(\) - IPv4 lookup
  ✓ molt_socket_gethostbyname_ex\(\) - IPv4 with aliases
  ✓ molt_socket_gethostbyaddr\(\) - reverse IP lookup
  ✓ molt_socket_getfqdn\(\) - FQDN resolution
  ✓ molt_socket_getservbyname\(\) - service port lookup
  ✓ molt_socket_getservbyport\(\) - service name lookup
  ✓ molt_socket_getprotobyname\(\) - protocol name lookup

BYTE ORDER:
  ✓ molt_socket_htons\(\) / molt_socket_ntohs\(\)
  ✓ molt_socket_htonl\(\) / molt_socket_ntohl\(\)

ADDRESS CONVERSION:
  ✓ molt_socket_inet_pton\(\) - string to binary address
  ✓ molt_socket_inet_ntop\(\) - binary to string address

MISCELLANEOUS:
  ✓ molt_socket_constants\(\) - get all constants dict
  ✓ molt_socket_has_ipv6\(\) - IPv6 support check
  ✓ molt_socket_has_dualstack_ipv6\(\) - IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack support
  ✓ molt_socket_if_nameindex\(\) - interface enumeration
  ✓ molt_socket_if_nametoindex\(\) - name to index
  ✓ molt_socket_if_indextoname\(\) - index to name
  ✓ molt_socket_cmsg_len\(\) - ancillary message space
  ✓ molt_socket_cmsg_space\(\) - ancillary with padding
  ✓ molt_socketpair\(\) - create paired sockets

SOCKET READER \(for makefile\):
  ✓ molt_socket_reader_new\(\) - create buffered reader
  ✓ molt_socket_reader_read\(\) - buffered read
  ✓ molt_socket_reader_readline\(\) - read line
  ✓ molt_socket_reader_readline_limit\(\) - read line with limit
  ✓ molt_socket_reader_drop\(\) - deallocate reader

TOTAL INTRINSIC FUNCTIONS: 62 exported, all core operations covered

================================================================================
7. DIFFERENTIAL TEST COVERAGE
================================================================================

TESTED AREAS \(70 test files\):
  ✓ Basic socket creation/operations \(socket_basic.py\)
  ✓ Constants and error handling \(socket_constants_errors.py, socket_eai_constants.py\)
  ✓ Address resolution \(socket_dns_basic.py, socket_getaddrinfo.py, socket_getnameinfo_basic.py\)
  ✓ Reverse DNS \(socket_reverse_dns.py, socket_gethostbyaddr_payload_intrinsic.py\)
  ✓ TCP/UDP communication \(socket_tcp_echo.py, socket_udp_echo.py\)
  ✓ IPv6 \(socket_ipv6_basic.py, socket_ipv6_flowinfo_scopeid.py\)
  ✓ Timeouts \(socket_timeout_accept.py, socket_timeout_recv.py, socket_setdefaulttimeout_validation.py\)
  ✓ Non-blocking operations \(socket_nonblocking_connect.py, socket_connect_ex_result.py\)
  ✓ Socket options \(socket_keepalive.py, socket_tcp_nodelay.py, socket_reuseaddr_reuseport.py\)
  ✓ File descriptors \(socket_fileno_detach_closed.py, socket_inheritable_dup_fromfd.py\)
  ✓ Socket pairs \(socket_socketpair_basic.py, socket_unix_socketpair_pathlike.py\)
  ✓ Message flags \(socket_msg_dontwait.py, socket_recv_peek.py\)
  ✓ Advanced I/O \(socket_sendmsg_recvmsg_basic.py, socket_recvmsg_into_basic.py, socket_sendmsg_ancillary_rights.py\)
  ✓ Interface lookups \(socket_if_name_interfaces.py\)
  ✓ Protocol/service lookups \(socket_getprotobyname.py, socket_service_lookup.py\)
  ✓ Auxiliary message helpers \(socket_cmsg_helpers.py\)
  ✓ Byte order \(socket_byteorder_and__socket_surface.py\)
  ✓ gethostbyname_ex \(socket_gethostbyname_ex.py\)
  ✓ makefile \(socket_makefile_basic.py, socket_makefile_readline_limit.py\)
  ✓ Error paths \(socket_error_paths.py, socket_closed_send_recv_errors.py\)
  ✓ Additional helpers \(socket_create_helpers.py, socket_inet_helpers.py\)

NOT YET TESTED:
  ✗ socket.share\(\) - Windows-only, not applicable on Unix
  ✗ socket.fromshare\(\) - Windows-only, not applicable on Unix
  ✗ socket.sendfile\(\) - not implemented
  ✗ socket.sendmsg_afalg\(\) - Linux AF_ALG crypto, not implemented
  ✗ sethostname\(\) - implemented as stub \(security-sensitive\)
  ✗ AddressFamily/SocketKind enum classes - not exported
  ✗ More exhaustive socket option coverage
  ✗ Platform-specific constants coverage gaps
  ✗ Edge cases in error handling for uncommon constants

================================================================================
8. GAP ANALYSIS SUMMARY
================================================================================

CATEGORIZED BY SEVERITY:

=== MISSING \(Completely absent\) ===

FUNCTIONS \(8 not exported\):
  1. close\(\) - module-level fd close \(low priority, rarely used directly\)
  2. gethostbyname_ex\(\) - available in _socket but not in main socket module __all__
  3. getprotobyname\(\) - available in _socket but not in main socket module __all__
  4. CMSG_LEN\(\) - available in _socket but not in main socket module __all__
  5. CMSG_SPACE\(\) - available in _socket but not in main socket module __all__
  6. if_nameindex\(\) - available in _socket but not in main socket module __all__
  7. if_nametoindex\(\) - available in _socket but not in main socket module __all__
  8. if_indextoname\(\) - available in _socket but not in main socket module __all__

NOTE: These 8 functions ARE IMPLEMENTED in the intrinsics and _socket module shim,
but they are NOT EXPORTED in socket.py's __all__ list. This is a DOCUMENTATION/EXPORT issue,
not an implementation gap.

SOCKET METHODS \(2\):
  1. sendfile\(\) - efficient file-to-socket transmission \(NOT IMPLEMENTED\)
     Impact: Low-medium \(optional, performance feature\)
  2. sendmsg_afalg\(\) - cryptographic socket operations \(NOT IMPLEMENTED\)
     Impact: Very low \(Linux-specific, specialized crypto use case\)

WINDOWS-ONLY \(not applicable on Unix\):
  1. share\(\) - socket sharing across processes \(Windows AF_SHARED\)
  2. fromshare\(\) - reconstruct from WSAPROTOCOL_INFO bytes

ENUM CLASSES \(3 - low priority\):
  1. AddressFamily - IntEnum wrapper around AF_* constants
  2. SocketKind - IntEnum wrapper around SOCK_* constants
  3. MsgFlag - IntFlag wrapper around MSG_* constants
  Impact: Low \(constants still accessible, affects type hints only\)

=== STUBBED \(Raise RuntimeError or incomplete\) ===

  1. sethostname\(\) - raises RuntimeError\("not implemented in Molt runtime yet"\)
     Reason: Security-sensitive, requires admin privileges
     Status: Intentional stub, reasonable for security

=== PARTIAL \(Implemented but may have edge cases\) ===

  1. Socket ancillary message handling \(recvmsg/sendmsg\)
     Status: Basic support exists, edge cases for padding/alignment may differ
  2. Socket option handling \(setsockopt/getsockopt\)
     Status: Works for common options, platform-specific options coverage may vary
  3. getaddrinfo\(\) flags
     Status: Core flags supported, edge cases with IDNA may differ
  4. UDP connect behavior
     Status: Supported but may differ on edge cases with invalid addresses
  5. Non-blocking socket errors and timeout behavior
     Status: Core paths tested, rare edge cases may differ

=== COMPLETE \(Should match CPython >= 3.12\) ===

  - All socket creation, binding, listening, connection methods
  - All send/recv variants \(basic + with addresses + with ancillary data\)
  - All socket options \(get/set\)
  - All DNS resolution functions
  - All byte-order conversion functions
  - All address conversion functions \(inet_pton/inet_ntop\)
  - Timeout and blocking mode management
  - Socket shutdown
  - File descriptor operations \(fileno, detach, dup, fromfd\)
  - makefile\(\) for file-like interface
  - Interface name lookup \(if_nameindex, if_nametoindex, if_indextoname\)
  - Protocol name lookup \(getprotobyname, via _socket\)
  - Service name/port lookup \(getservbyname, getservbyport\)
  - Auxiliary message functions \(CMSG_LEN, CMSG_SPACE, via _socket\)
  - File descriptor passing \(send_fds, recv_fds\)
  - IPv6 support detection \(has_ipv6, has_dualstack_ipv6\)
  - Constants \(197+ AF/SOCK/IPPROTO/SOL/SO/MSG/AI/NI/EAI/SHUT/IP/IPV6/TCP/etc.\)
  - Exception classes \(error, gaierror, herror, timeout\)

================================================================================
9. ACTIONABLE RECOMMENDATIONS
================================================================================

CRITICAL \(Fixes socket completeness\):
  [ ] Export missing functions in socket.py __all__:
      - Add 'gethostbyname_ex' \(easy, already implemented in _socket\)
      - Add 'getprotobyname' \(easy, already implemented in _socket\)
      - Add 'CMSG_LEN' \(easy, already implemented in _socket\)
      - Add 'CMSG_SPACE' \(easy, already implemented in _socket\)
      - Add 'if_nameindex' \(easy, already implemented in _socket\)
      - Add 'if_nametoindex' \(easy, already implemented in _socket\)
      - Add 'if_indextoname' \(easy, already implemented in _socket\)
      - Add 'close' \(module-level, via molt_os_close\)
      NOTE: These require importing from _socket or calling intrinsics directly

HIGH \(Improves type safety and stdlib parity\):
  [ ] Export enum classes \(AddressFamily, SocketKind, MsgFlag\)
      - Can be simple IntEnum wrappers around constants
      - Improves type hints and IDE support
  [ ] Implement sendfile\(\) method for socket class
      - Check if platform/runtime supports it
      - Add efficient file-to-network transmission
  [ ] Add tests for newly exported functions

MEDIUM \(Optional, platform-specific\):
  [ ] Investigate Windows socket.share\(\)/fromshare\(\) support
      - Lower priority on Unix-only platforms
      - May require special WSAPROTOCOL_INFO handling
  [ ] Implement sendmsg_afalg\(\) for AF_ALG crypto sockets
      - Very niche use case
      - Linux-specific ALG socket family required

LOW \(Edge case handling\):
  [ ] Expand test coverage for:
      - Rare socket option combinations
      - Edge cases in getaddrinfo with unusual flags
      - Platform-specific constant coverage
      - Error handling in less common scenarios
  [ ] Document known limitations vs CPython
  [ ] Implement sethostname\(\) if needed \(currently stub for security\)

================================================================================
10. FINAL STATUS
================================================================================

OVERALL ASSESSMENT: ~95% COMPLETE for primary use cases

Public API Coverage:
  - Module-level functions: 19/27 exported \(70%\), but 8 more available via _socket
  - Socket class methods: 30/32 implemented \(94%\)
    * Missing: sendfile\(\), sendmsg_afalg\(\) \(specialized, optional\)
    * Complete: All core socket operations
  - Constants: 197+ available \(100%\)
  - Exceptions: All 4 types available \(100%\)
  - Enum classes: Not exported \(3 missing\) - low priority

Intrinsic Implementations: 62 functions - ALL CORE OPERATIONS COVERED

Test Coverage: 70 differential tests covering major functionality areas

Recommendation: Mark socket as "mostly complete" with minor export/export issues.
The 8 functions not in socket.py __all__ are available via _socket module and
intrinsics - this is primarily a PUBLIC API DOCUMENTATION issue rather than
an implementation gap.

EOF
cat /tmp/molt_socket_audit.txt)
Bash(ruff check src/molt/stdlib/socket.py src/molt/stdlib/_socket.py && ruff format --check src/molt/stdlib/socket.py src/molt/stdlib/_socket.py 2>&1)
